steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content:
Since both equations equal $y$, set the right-hand sides equal to each other:
$$3x = x^2 - 28$$

steps.title: Solve and apply the constraint
steps.content:
Rearrange to standard form:
$$x^2 - 3x - 28 = 0$$
Factor by finding two numbers that multiply to $-28$ and add to $-3$. Those are $-7$ and $4$:
$$(x - 7)(x + 4) = 0$$
So $x = 7$ or $x = -4$. Since we need $x > 0$, the answer is $x = 7$.
